name: Linear Search + Slicing
summary: Take preorder[0] as the root, linear-scan inorder to find it, then recurse on sliced left/right sub-arrays — O(n^2).
visualizationIntent: init: the stage starts empty; the current preorder and inorder sub-arrays appear as aux strips. build: preorder[0] is the current root — a node pops in and links to its parent; a linear scan sweeps inorder to find that root (the O(n) cost, made visible), splitting it into left and right sub-arrays that are COPIED for the recursive calls, so the aux strips visibly shrink as we descend. recurse: build the left sub-array's subtree, then the right. done: the finished tree is serialized to level-order as the result.

lineExplanations.3: Start an empty list of node objects for the drawing.
lineExplanations.4: Start a lookup from a node's id to its object.
lineExplanations.6: A one-slot holder for the next node id (a creation counter).
lineExplanations.7: Build the whole tree from the full arrays; get the root id.
lineExplanations.8: Serialize the finished tree to level-order form.
lineExplanations.12: Base case: an empty sub-array — no node here.
lineExplanations.13: Return None for an empty sub-array.
lineExplanations.14: The first preorder value is this subtree's root.
lineExplanations.15: Take the next free node id.
lineExplanations.17: Create the root node with empty child links.
lineExplanations.18: Add the new node to the tree.
lineExplanations.19: Register it under its id for fast lookup.
lineExplanations.20: Mark it building (on the call stack).
lineExplanations.21: Does this node have a parent to attach to?
lineExplanations.22: Is it the parent's left child?
lineExplanations.23: Attach it as the parent's left child.
lineExplanations.25: Otherwise attach it as the parent's right child.
lineExplanations.26: Linear-scan inorder to find the root's index.
lineExplanations.27: Everything before it is the left subtree's size.
lineExplanations.28: Slice the left subtree's preorder values.
lineExplanations.29: Slice the left subtree's inorder values.
lineExplanations.30: Recursively build the left subtree from those slices.
lineExplanations.31: Slice the right subtree's preorder values.
lineExplanations.32: Slice the right subtree's inorder values.
lineExplanations.34: Both subtrees built — mark this node done.
lineExplanations.35: Return this node's id to its parent.
lineExplanations.39: Scan while there are elements left.
lineExplanations.40: Is this the value we are looking for?
lineExplanations.42: Not yet — move to the next element.
lineExplanations.46: Start a queue to walk the tree breadth-first.
lineExplanations.47: Seed the walk with the root if the tree is non-empty.
lineExplanations.50: Take the next id from the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.51: A None entry marks a missing child.
lineExplanations.52: Emit null for that missing child.
lineExplanations.57: Trim trailing nulls the level-order format omits.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def buildTree(self, preorder, inorder): the entry method; the two traversal arrays.
syntaxExplanations.4: by_id = {} creates an empty dict (id -> node).
syntaxExplanations.5: state_by_id = [] will hold one status per node id.
syntaxExplanations.6: next_id = [0] is a one-element list used as a mutable counter.
syntaxExplanations.7: self.build(...) constructs the tree and returns the root id.
syntaxExplanations.8: self.serialize(...) turns the tree into a level-order list.
syntaxExplanations.9: return result hands back the answer.
syntaxExplanations.11: def build(self, preorder, inorder, ...): builds the subtree described by these sub-arrays.
syntaxExplanations.13: return None: no node for empty input.
syntaxExplanations.14: preorder[0] is this subtree's root value.
syntaxExplanations.15: node_id = next_id[0] takes the next free id.
syntaxExplanations.17: A dict literal builds the node object.
syntaxExplanations.18: nodes.append(node) adds it to the drawing.
syntaxExplanations.21: if parent_id != -1: skip linking for the overall root.
syntaxExplanations.22: if side == 0: it is a left child.
syntaxExplanations.24: else: it is a right child.
syntaxExplanations.26: self.indexOf(...) linear-scans inorder for the root.
syntaxExplanations.27: left_size = root_index: nodes before the root go left.
syntaxExplanations.28: preorder[1:1+left_size] slices the left preorder values.
syntaxExplanations.29: inorder[0:root_index] slices the left inorder values.
syntaxExplanations.30: self.build(...) recurses on the left slices.
syntaxExplanations.31: preorder[1+left_size:] slices the right preorder values.
syntaxExplanations.32: inorder[root_index+1:] slices the right inorder values.
syntaxExplanations.33: self.build(...) recurses on the right slices.
syntaxExplanations.35: return node_id gives the parent this subtree's root.
syntaxExplanations.37: def indexOf(self, inorder, target): returns target's index in inorder.
syntaxExplanations.38: i = 0 starts the scan at the front.
syntaxExplanations.39: while i < len(inorder): scan until found.
syntaxExplanations.40: if inorder[i] == target: is this the value?
syntaxExplanations.41: return i hands back the found index.
syntaxExplanations.42: i = i + 1 moves to the next element.
syntaxExplanations.44: def serialize(self, root, by_id): BFS the tree into a level-order list.
syntaxExplanations.46: out_q = [] is a list used as a queue.
syntaxExplanations.47: if root is not None: only walk a non-empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.49: while len(out_q) > 0: until the queue empties.
syntaxExplanations.51: if emit_id is None: a missing child.
syntaxExplanations.57: while ... is None: trim trailing nulls.
syntaxExplanations.59: return result hands back the level-order array.
